p1_string_of_nickels.physical_facts: Dense foliage, circular leaves, consistent yellow pot occupancy.
p1_string_of_nickels.explanatory_transformations: Maintained stable growth throughout the sequence; no significant leaf drop or elongation.
p1_string_of_nickels.visual_health_reasoning: Healthy; turgor pressure appears optimal with no chlorosis.

p2_mexican_mint.physical_facts: Two primary leaves, central position, secondary pair emerging.
p2_mexican_mint.explanatory_transformations: Gradual decline in leaf turgor observed from T-2 to Current; leaf margins show slight curling.
p2_mexican_mint.visual_health_reasoning: Stressed; leaf-margin necrosis and downward curling suggest potential over-watering or root-zone hypoxia.

p3_pothos.physical_facts: Two leaves, white rabbit anchor present.
p3_pothos.explanatory_transformations: The apical leaf has maintained a consistent orientation relative to the rabbit; no significant growth or senescence.
p3_pothos.visual_health_reasoning: Stable; leaf integrity is maintained despite the small hole observed in the earliest image.

p4_silver_guest.physical_facts: Small seedling near rim of p2 pot.
p4_silver_guest.explanatory_transformations: Visible wilting and loss of verticality compared to the earliest image.
p4_silver_guest.visual_health_reasoning: Critical; the seedling shows signs of damping-off or severe dehydration.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Soil in p2/p4 appears consistently damp with a darkening hue, suggesting high moisture retention.
biome_observations.incidental_growth: No new weeds or moss detected; p4 remains the only secondary seedling.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Debris (small organic matter) remains static on the soil surface of p2.

temporal_deltas.summary: The sequence shows a transition from a stable state to a decline in the p2/p4 pot, while p1 and p3 remain static.

visual_health_inference.reasoning: P1 and P3 are thriving. P2 and P4 are showing signs of physiological stress, likely due to soil moisture management issues in the shared pot.

vision_report.anomalies: The p2/p4 pot shows a persistent dark, damp soil patch that has not dried out over the 5-day observation period.
vision_report.narrative_description: The botanical environment is bifurcated: the established plants (p1, p3) are stable, while the younger specimens (p2, p4) are struggling with environmental conditions, likely excess soil moisture.
